title: Best Buy Co., Inc. (BBY) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Best Buy (BBY) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 3% FCF growth rate is projected. This modest growth aligns with the expectation that Best Buy's traditional retail operations will experience slow to flat revenue growth, partially offset by margin expansion from its growing high-margin services (like Geek Squad) and careful cost management.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A discount rate of 9% is applied. This reflects the relatively higher risks associated with the traditional retail sector, the cyclicality of consumer electronics demand, and competitive threats from e-commerce giants.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2% terminal growth rate conservatively assumes the company will grow in line with long-term inflation and slightly below historical GDP growth, reflecting the mature nature of its business.

thesisParagraphs: Best Buy has successfully navigated the shift to digital retail better than many of its historical peers. Its network of over 1,000 stores serves not just as retail outlets, but as fulfillment hubs and service centers for the complex consumer electronics ecosystem.

The ongoing integration of Geek Squad services and membership programs provides a buffer against pure-play e-commerce competitors. While revenue growth may be constrained in the near term, strong cash generation, a healthy dividend, and share repurchases offer a degree of downside protection. The current market price suggests the stock is moderately undervalued relative to its cash-generating potential, assuming it can maintain its market share.
verdictFooter: Based on actuals and standard projections. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why a 3% FCF growth rate for Best Buy?
faq.answer: While top-line sales face pressure from e-commerce, Best Buy's shift toward high-margin services and efficient capital management is expected to support a modest 3% free cash flow growth rate.

faq.question: What justifies the 9% discount rate?
faq.answer: The 9% discount rate accounts for the inherent risks of brick-and-mortar retail in a digital age and the cyclical nature of consumer spending on large electronics.

faq.question: Is Best Buy fundamentally sound?
faq.answer: Yes. Despite retail challenges, Best Buy generates solid free cash flow, possesses a strong brand, and operates a specialized service network that is difficult for purely online retailers to replicate.
